title: [phase:replan] [family:reporting] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Produce a concise operator-facing checkpoint: what is done, what remains blocked, and the next safest action. No edits.
summary_text: - `go install` can drift unless version-pinned (supply-chain/repro risk); a new upstream `nlm` release could silently break adapter expectations. - Install does not solve the documented hard gate that `nlm` auth must already exist on the runner user; you can still fail runtime smoke after successful install. - `go install` default output (`$GOPATH/bin`, often `~/go/bin`) is not in your explicit fallback search list in `scripts/lib/notebooklm-bin.sh`, so binary resolution can still fail if `PATH` is not updated in-job. - Adding networked tool bootstrap to the protected runtime job increases failure modes (GitHub/Go proxy/module fetch outages) versus using a pre-provisioned runner binary. - If a preinstalled `nlm` already exists, path precedence can select the wrong binary version (old vs newly installed), making failures nondeterministic and harder to triage.
